sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.23 (Apr 24 15:19 tag, spreading Apr 24-25)** — Adds **image gen + reference-image editing via Codex OAuth** to Providers/OpenAI (no OPENAI_API_KEY needed for `openai/gpt-image-2`). Adds **image gen + reference-image editing via the `image_generate` API** to Providers/OpenRouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY runs OpenRouter image models). Following v2026.4.22's xAI image gen / TTS / STT expansion, the image-gen stack is now parallelized across 3 providers (xAI / OpenAI / OpenRouter).

citations.label: Releasebot - OpenClaw Release Notes April 2026

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.10.0 (Apr 16) onward, no major release inside the 3-day window** — No new version Apr 22-25. Continued Nous Research activity.

citations.label: The New Stack - OpenClaw vs. Hermes Agent

bullets.body: **NemoClaw (NVIDIA)** — No new major update inside the 3-day window. The OpenShell + sandbox / policy controls announced at GTC 2026 keep rolling out.

citations.label: Open Source AI Agents 2026: OpenClaw vs Hermes vs Nemoclaw

bullets.body: **Claude Code v2.1.117 (released Apr 25)** — `/resume` **auto-summarizes stalled / large sessions before reload to prevent context overflow**, aligned with `--resume` behavior. A model selection different from the project pin persists across restarts; boot header shows model source ("project pin" / "managed-settings pin"). Adds `CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1` to enable forking subagent via external builds; supports auto-loading agent frontmatter `mcpServers` via `--agent`.

citations.label: Claude Code Changelog (claudefa.st)

bullets.body: **Anthropic Claude Code v2.1.116 (Apr 20) quality-recovery follow-on improvements (Apr 22-24)** — Vim visual mode (`v`) / visual-line mode (`V`), `/cost` and `/stats` consolidated under `/usage`, `/theme` for custom theme creation / switching, direct JSON editing of `~/.claude/themes/`. The 3 reverts in v2.1.116 (Apr 20) — reasoning effort high → medium default change / session idle thinking-clear bug / verbosity-reduction prompt — are all complete.

citations.label: Releasebot - Claude Code by Anthropic

bullets.body: **OpenAI GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 Pro API public on Apr 24 (ChatGPT / Codex public on Apr 23)** — Rolled out to ChatGPT Plus / Pro / Business / Enterprise and Codex on Apr 23; API public **on Apr 24**. Input $5.00 / output $30.00 per M tokens (**2x increase** vs GPT-5.4); GPT-5.5 Pro at $30 / $180 per M tokens, same as GPT-5.4 Pro. **Codex cuts output tokens ~40%** to "offset the per-token raise via per-task cost."

citations.label: Apidog - GPT-5.5 Pricing: Full Breakdown of API, Codex, and ChatGPT Costs

bullets.body: **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Managed Harness + CLI + Skills detail (Apr 22 spreading)** — AgentCore CLI is **AWS CDK-native, with Terraform support coming soon**; Coding Skills are **shipping today on Kiro Power, with Claude Code / Codex / Cursor next week** — staged rollout. Managed Harness is in 4-region preview (US West (Oregon) / US East (N. Virginia) / Europe (Frankfurt) / Asia Pacific (Sydney)); CLI is GA in 14 regions, no extra charge.

citations.label: AWS Blog - Get to your first working agent in minutes

The third Google Cloud frontier-lab deal after Anthropic and Meta, announced at Google Cloud Next '26.

Agentic above Claude Sonnet 4.5, self-rated Opus 4.5-class. Reasoning "marginally short" vs GPT-5.4 / Gemini 3.1-Pro.

sections.category: AI Security > CVE / KEV / Threat Intelligence

bullets.body: **CISA adds 4 to KEV on Apr 24 (Samsung MagicINFO 9 / SimpleHelp x2 / D-Link DIR-823X) + Federal remediation deadline May 8** — CVE-2024-7399 (Samsung MagicINFO 9 Path Traversal, CVSS 8.8), CVE-2024-57726 (SimpleHelp Missing Authorization, CVSS 9.9), CVE-2024-57728 (SimpleHelp Path Traversal, CVSS 7.2), CVE-2025-29635 (D-Link DIR-823X Command Injection) added as "actively exploited"; FCEB must remediate **by 2026-05-08**, D-Link must be discontinued.

citations.label: CISA - CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog

bullets.body: **Windows Forum: patch Samsung MagicINFO / SimpleHelp / D-Link immediately (Apr 24)** — Enterprise-targeted explainer of the 4 KEV adds.

citations.label: Windows Forum - CISA Adds 4 KEV Flaws: Patch Samsung MagicINFO, SimpleHelp, D-Link ASAP

bullets.body: **Nginx UI CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8) actively exploited in the wild** — `/mcp_message` endpoint with empty default IP allowlist bypasses the auth middleware, exposing 12 unauthenticated MCP tool calls → 2 HTTP requests to fully take over Nginx. Pluto Security names it **MCPwn**; Recorded Future lists it among Top 31 actively exploited CVEs in March 2026; Shodan shows **2,600+ instances exposed**; fixed in v2.3.4.

citations.label: The Hacker News - Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover

bullets.body: **Picus Security: root cause analysis of CVE-2026-33032 missing middleware (April)** — A missing `AuthRequired()` middleware call on `/mcp_message`, combined with "empty IP list = allow all," is the root cause.

citations.label: Picus Security - CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn): How a Missing Middleware Call in nginx-ui Hands Attackers Full Web Server Takeover

bullets.body: **Dark Reading: Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk** — A first-of-its-kind industry case for "MCP-protocol-integration vulnerability."

citations.label: Dark Reading - Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk

bullets.body: **BleepingComputer: Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild** — Recap of the attack state, scope, and mitigations.

citations.label: BleepingComputer - Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild

bullets.body: **Saltcorn CVE-2026-41478 (CVSS 9.9, disclosed Apr 24)** — Mobile-Sync feature SQL injection lets an authenticated low-privilege user inject arbitrary SQL, leaking / tampering with the full DB including admin password hashes / config secrets. Fixed in 1.4.6 / 1.5.6 / 1.6.0-beta.5.

citations.label: TheHackerWire - Saltcorn Mobile-Sync SQL Injection (CVE-2026-41478)

bullets.body: **Delta Electronics AS320T CVE-2026-1949 / CVE-2026-1950 (CVSS 9.8, disclosed Apr 24)** — Industrial automation gear, stack buffer overflow / filename-handling critical defects.

citations.label: TheHackerWire - Delta Electronics AS320T Critical Stack Buffer Overflow (CVE-2026-1949)

sections.category: AI Security > Solutions: Zero-trust Access Control for Agentic AI

bullets.body: **Okta for AI Agents (GA 2026-04-30, final-week countdown)** — Registers agents in a central directory as first-class identities, with continuous discovery of sanctioned / shadow AI, an Agent Gateway as the central control plane consolidating MCP virtual servers / Okta MCP registry, credential vaulting / auto-rotation, and runtime enforcement via universal logout. With GA 5 days out, enterprise migration guides and integration cases are surfacing in volume around Apr 25. CEO Todd McKinnon calls it "the company's most important product."

citations.label: HyperFRAME Research - Identity as the Last Firewall: Analyzing Okta for AI Agents

bullets.body: **Microsoft Copilot Studio CVE-2026-21520 (ShareLeak) aftermath** — Existing patch is applied, but indirect prompt injection via SharePoint forms is not fully closed by patches alone, per Capsule Security. The conclusion: enterprises must audit MCP / agent connection points. Microsoft assigning a CVE to an agentic platform is described as "exceptional" and likely to set an industry precedent.

citations.label: Welcome.AI - Microsoft's Copilot Studio Vulnerability Highlights Risks for Enterprises

bullets.body: **PointGuard AI: CVE-2026-21520 Copilot Studio Information Disclosure detail** — Frames it as an "over-sharing assistant" — a structural weakness in agentic-product design.

citations.label: PointGuard AI - CVE-2026-21520 Copilot Studio Information Disclosure

bullets.body: **NVIDIA retakes $5.12T market cap on Apr 24 close, gap to Alphabet widens past $1T** — Intel's after-close beat on Apr 23 lights up the chip sector. NVIDIA closes at a record $208.27 (+4.3%). Q1 FY27 print is **May 20**, with consensus calling for **3-digit EPS growth / +77% revenue YoY**. Wall Street's stance: "Own It, Don't Trade It."

citations.label: Benzinga - Nvidia Tops $5 Trillion Market Cap As AI Boom Lifts Intel, AMD And Chip Stocks

bullets.body: **AMD +13.90% closes at $347.77, May 5 print rally (Apr 24)** — Q1 2026 print estimates: revenue $9.84B, EPS YoY +33%. The Intel beat + NVIDIA $5T sector chain accelerates the chained move; AI chip supply-easing is also a contributor.

**BMW Group begins serious AEON humanoid trials at Plant Leipzig from April 2026 + opens Physical AI Center of Competence** — Building on Spartanburg's **Figure 02 record (30,000 X3 vehicles supported in 10 months, 90,000 parts moved / 1.2M steps / 1,250 hours running)**, BMW opens Europe's first Physical AI hub in Leipzig. Hexagon Robotics' **AEON** (22 sensors / self-swap battery / 4-layer Physical AI / autonomous behavior learned in 20 demos) enters serious trial **from April 2026, on the path to a summer pilot**. Planned for high-voltage battery assembly / parts manufacturing.

citations.label: BMW Group Press - BMW Group bringing Physical AI to Europe / Pilot project at BMW Group Plant Leipzig
